item.title: The whisperer in darkness
item.description: Bear in mind closely that I did not see any actual visual horror at the end. To say that a mental shock was the cause of what I inferred—that last straw which sent me racing out of the lonely Akeley farmhouse and through the wild domed hills of Vermont in a commandeered motor at night—is to ignore the plainest facts of my final experience. Notwithstanding the deep things I saw and heard, and the admitted vividness the impression produced on me by these things, I cannot prove even now whether I was right or wrong in my hideous inference.

item.title: Lorem ipsum
item.description: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Vestibulum eget porta quam. Sed ac mi massa. Donec cursus, nisi a blandit volutpat, nisi ante ultricies ante, vel gravida ante diam non quam. Vestibulum fermentum mauris ac consectetur sagittis. Pellentesque id nulla vel tellus sodales mollis. Duis pulvinar urna eget euismod lacinia. Vestibulum varius tristique lacus bibendum euismod. Nam ante ante, imperdiet ut iaculis sit amet, gravida at magna. In sit amet nunc in orci aliquam viverra.

item.title: CRIRES+ at CS 20.5
item.description: To be updated

item.title: about
item.description: Who am I? I grew up in Southern France, where the Pyrénées mountain range meet the Mediterranean sea, also where there is the biggest oak barrel in the world. I studied in Perpignan 🇫🇷, Marseille 🇫🇷, Uppsala 🇸🇪, Paris/Meudon/Orsay 🇫🇷. I shortly worked at the European Space Agency in Noordwijk 🇳🇱, qualifying the Descent Camera for the Exomars 2016 lander (which made an impressive crater on Mars) before completing a PhD in astronomy at Uppsala University 🇸🇪 with a 1-year studentship at the European Southern Observatory in Garching near München 🇩🇪🍺.

item.title: cv
item.description: Here you can download my academic CV (PDF document) The list of my publications can be found at this link (redirects to the ADS).

item.title: #20 Swedish-hopped farmhouse ale
item.description: Brew date: 2020-11-30 Brewer: alexis Malt budget 1000 g wheat 1500 g light munich 2000 g pilsner Strike water 20 L at 75 deg, with some juniper branches Infused in a bucket with the grain, 68 deg. 11:40. Kept under wool blanket Warming 10 L sparge water Hops to chose from: SWE26 Frebo 3.9 %AA <- SWE51 Klev 6.1 %AA <- SWE52 Brusbo 2.8 %AA SWE08 Spångby SWE?

item.title: research
item.description: Research interests Transit spectroscopy and exoplanetary atmospheres Magnetism of cool stars High-resolution near-infrared spectroscopy High-resolution optical spectropolarimetry and Zeeman-Doppler imaging Instrumentation Refereed publications The large-scale magnetic field of the eccentric pre-main-sequence binary system V1878 Ori. Lavail, A., Kochukhov, O., Hussain, G., et al. 2020, MNRAS, 497, 632. ADS | DOI Characterising the surface magnetic fields of T Tauri stars with high-resolution near-infrared spectroscopy. Lavail, A.

item.title: PhD defense
item.description: 2020-05-26 | Uppsala University (Sweden) The day I got my PhD, also my birthday if you want to know. Slides from my presentation 🠔 back to presentations

item.title: AD Leo. Observations from 2019
item.description: LSD Profiles We got 6 observations in 2019. Stokes V profiles shown below. It looks like amplitude-wise, the profiles are recovered most of the pre-2016 amplitude. Stokes V profiles The phase coverage is not great, we have four observations clustered at phase 0.7-0.8, one at 0.0, one around 0.5: 0.00 0.46 2.72 1.80 1.81 1.81 ZDI I ran ZDI on the data anyhow, following same approach than for 2012 and 2016 data, with the same Stokes I filling factor value, regularisation parameters values, and Stokes V filling factor values.

item.title: A logo proposal for Svenska astronomiska sällskapet
contact
item.description: If you’d like to get in touch / have any question, feel free to contact me! E-mail LinkedIn Visiting adress: room Å95123, Ångströmlaboratoriet, Lägerhyddsvägen 1, Uppsala Post adress: Alexis Lavail, Box 516, 75120 Uppsala (Sweden)

item.title: Presentations from the Uppsala-Stockholm PhD days
item.description: 10-11 april 2014, Uppsala Universitet Thursday 10 april 2014 Kai Yan Lee - Reionization and the cosmic dawn Hannes Jensen - Simulating observational probes of reionization Saghar Asadi - Gravitational millilensing as a probe of dark matter on subgalactic scales Beatriz Villarroel Rodriguez - What is an Active Galactic Nuclei? Sarah Jabbari - Origin of solar surface activity and sunspots Alexis Lavail - Investigation of stellar magnetic fields using infrared spectropolarimetry Lisa Rosén - Magnetic fields of cool active stars Naum Rusomarov - Magnetic fields of Ap stars from all four Stokes parameters Friday 11 april 2014 Andreas Sandberg - Observations of Lyman alpha emitting galaxies at low and intermediate redshifts Florent Duval - Lyman alpha radiative transfer in Star-burst Galaxies Johannes Puschnig - Multi-wavelength studies of star-forming galaxies Thøger Juul Rivera-Thorsen - Neutral gas kinematics and geometry in young, starforming galaxies and its impact on Lyman alpha radiation transport Samuel Regandell - Spectral Synthesis for protoplanetary disk models Gianni Cataldi - Observations and modeling of debris disks Sara Lindgren - Accurate spectroscopy of M dwarfs, a possible link to understand planet formation Erik Aronson - Spectroscopic characterization of exoplanetary atmospheres

item.title: STARRY conference
item.description: 2019-06-18 🠖 2019-06-21 | Leeds (UK) Conference website Slides from my presentation 🠔 back to presentations

item.title: A sudden change of the global magnetic field of the active M-dwarf AD Leo
item.description: Alexis Lavail, Oleg Kochukhov, and Gregg Wade A poster presented at the Cool Stars 20 conference Useful links PDF version of the poster Link to the paper on ADS Summary of the science in not-so-rigorous language Our aim was to get the first observations ever of a M-dwarf in all four Stokes parameters. This means one gets the intensity spectrum (Stokes I), the circularly polarized spectrum (Stokes V) and the two linearly polarized spectra (Stokes Q and U).
